key_insight: A self-attention neural surrogate can learn PFEM free-surface fluid dynamics directly on evolving meshes, enabling accurate, geometry-aware rollouts at much lower cost.
engineering_relevance: This offers a scalable way to replace repeated high-fidelity PFEM solves in engineering problems with moving boundaries, nonlinear materials, and long-horizon predictions.
method: Preserve PFEM mesh discretization and use attention to model node-to-node interactions on arbitrary evolving geometries.
Compare standard self-attention with a linear attention variant to reduce computational cost and improve scalability.
Use the mesh-based output to reconstruct derived quantities such as stress fields with finite element operators.
evidence_signal: The models are evaluated on 2D and 3D free-surface flow benchmarks with evolving geometries, varying material parameters, and non-Newtonian fluids, showing accurate transient and final-state predictions.
watch_item: Performance depends on robust remeshing and node redistribution during rollouts, which may be challenging for highly complex or highly deforming flows.
paper_title: Attention mechanism for scalable mesh-based neural surrogates of free-surface fluids
